scenario: CompanyA operates in different regions and the currency exchange rate varies on a daily basis. The company plans to integrate the currency exchange rate API from a third-party provider. You need to design a custom connector that retrieves the latest exchange rates for all currencies.
q: Which three components should you use? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
why: To build the custom connector you define: an authentication type (how the connector authenticates to the third-party exchange-rate API, e.g. API key), an OpenAPI definition (the connector's actions/operations and schema describing the API), and an HTTP verb (the method, e.g. GET, used by the operation that retrieves the rates). A 'test connector' is not a build component (testing happens after creation), and an app registration (Entra) is only needed for OAuth/AAD-secured APIs, not required here.
